statement.p: A farmer purchased 520 meters of fencing, and will build a rectangular pen with it. To enclose the largest possible area, what should the pens length and width be? Model the pens area with a function, and then find its maximum value.
Use a comma to separate your answers.
To enclose the largest possible area, the pens length and width should be meters.

solution.p: Assume the rectangles length is l meters, and its width is w meters. Since the rectangles perimeter is given as 520 meters, we have:
The area of the pen is A=lw\text{.} Substitute w=260-l into A=lw\text{,} we have:
Next, we use technology to graph the function A=-l^2+260l\text{,} and then find the parabolas vertex (130,{16900})\text{.}
According to the vertex point (130,{16900})\text{,} when the pens length is 130 meters, the pen has the maximum area {16900} square meters.
To maximize the pens area, the length and width should both be 130 meters. This implies the pen is a square.
